raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.7,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.6","R1.7","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"Reviewing the section centrally includes assessing whether the contribution framing and thesis claims are appropriately constructed.","R1.2":"The delegation entails checking whether the argument moves coherently from routing design to empirical results to deployment claims.","R1.3":"The reported compute reduction and deviation figures must be checked against the strength of the claims they are used to support.","R1.4":"Claims of a substantial advance, state of the art, and new standard make novelty assessment central to the review responsibility.","R1.6":"A section-level review normally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, register, and concision.","R1.7":"The artifact relies on named climate ensembles, assessment reports, and scenario frameworks, making citation and scholarship review central.","RX.1":"The reviewer must disclose uncertainty where the artifact lacks enough information to verify technical or scholarly claims.","RX.2":"The section contains strong claims about fundamental shifts, state of the art, and deployment, so overclaim avoidance is load-bearing.","RX.3":"The projection must remain limited to identifying review responsibilities rather than performing the review itself.","RX.4":"Because the artifact concerns climate decision-support for infrastructure planning, downstream-harm avoidance is load-bearing.","RX.5":"Traceability to the artifact's stated claims, metrics, baselines, and citations is necessary for a valid responsibility projection."}}

rationale.R1.1: Reviewing the section centrally includes assessing whether the contribution framing and thesis claims are appropriately constructed.
rationale.R1.2: The delegation entails checking whether the argument moves coherently from routing design to empirical results to deployment claims.
rationale.R1.3: The reported compute reduction and deviation figures must be checked against the strength of the claims they are used to support.
rationale.R1.4: Claims of a substantial advance, state of the art, and new standard make novelty assessment central to the review responsibility.
rationale.R1.6: A section-level review normally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, register, and concision.
rationale.R1.7: The artifact relies on named climate ensembles, assessment reports, and scenario frameworks, making citation and scholarship review central.
rationale.RX.1: The reviewer must disclose uncertainty where the artifact lacks enough information to verify technical or scholarly claims.
rationale.RX.2: The section contains strong claims about fundamental shifts, state of the art, and deployment, so overclaim avoidance is load-bearing.
rationale.RX.3: The projection must remain limited to identifying review responsibilities rather than performing the review itself.
rationale.RX.4: Because the artifact concerns climate decision-support for infrastructure planning, downstream-harm avoidance is load-bearing.
rationale.RX.5: Traceability to the artifact's stated claims, metrics, baselines, and citations is necessary for a valid responsibility projection.
